sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Tyler, a 56-year-old male, presents with a 4-5 week history of difficulty swallowing.
He describes a sensation of something being stuck in his throat, which is occasionally painful and requires him to wash it down with liquids.
He has never fully choked but has had thoughts about potentially choking.
The symptoms are more pronounced with solids than liquids.
Past medical history: Diabetes and high blood pressure.
He is on metformin 500mg twice a day for his diabetes and norvasc for his high blood pressure.
Review of systems: No coughing, blood in stool or vomit, weight loss, chest pain, or shortness of breath.
Current medications:
Metformin 500mg twice a day for diabetes
Norvasc for high blood pressure (quantity not specified)
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/50, temperature not mentioned, pulse rate not mentioned, oxygen saturation not mentioned.

Physical exam: Neck is fine with no swelling, thyroid feels normal, no masses or lymph nodes, throat examination reveals no masses or swelling, carotid bruit absent, lung exam clear, heart exam normal, belly exam reveals epigastric tenderness on the right, back exam normal, extremity exam normal, neurological exam fine.
Test Results: None mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Difficulty swallowing, suspected narrowing of the esophagus possibly due to stricture, diabetes, hypertension.
Plan:
I prescribed prilosec 20mg once a day for reflux.
I recommended chewing foods frequently and thoroughly, especially hard foods like steak or meat, to prevent them from getting stuck.
I referred Tyler to gastroenterology for a scope to investigate the cause of his difficulty swallowing.
I ordered a hemoglobin A1c and kidney function tests.
I also referred Tyler to ophthalmology for an eye examination for diabetic retinopathy.
I will continue his metformin and norvasc as before.
Follow-up:
I will see Tyler in about a month to review the results of the blood tests and make any necessary adjustments to his treatment plan.
I will also refill his norvasc prescription.
